location: Guaranteed drop from defeating the NPC boss Red Bear inside the Northern Nameless Mausoleum, a small dungeon atop an isolated plateau. Reached from Temple Town Ruins by heading east/up to a sealed Spiritspring (break the stone seal on the path off to the right), then taking two consecutive Spiritspring jumps up from the Rauh Base area to the mausoleum.
tactics: Unlock the sealed Spiritspring near Temple Town Ruins first (destroy the stone cairn blocking it), then chain two Spiritspring jumps to reach the mausoleum plateau.
Red Bear attacks fast in bursts (2-claw combo escalating to a 4-claw combo, plus a leaping ground slam) but closes distance slowly — punish the pause after his combos end.
His big attack, the Roar of Rugalea, is a two-hit wide-area nuke — watch for the wind-up and get clear or block both hits; he never runs out of stamina, so patience matters.

Don't confuse him with Ralva/Rugalea the Great Red Bear fought elsewhere in the DLC — Red Bear is a humanoid Tarnished wearing that creature's pelt and claws as gear, not the giant bear itself. He can only heal once, after which the damage race favors you.
lore: These claws once belonged to a great red bear before a lone Redmane warrior killed it in a brutal one-on-one duel. Rather than refashion his trophy into a proper weapon, he wields the claws exactly as they were, having abandoned his own name and identity to chase the beast's raw, untamed strength.

builds.name: Pure Strength Bleed Build
builds.why: C-tier Strength scaling (versus a weak E in Dexterity) with a fixed 45 blood loss buildup rewards dumping levels straight into Strength; the weapon can't be infused or buffed, so raw stat scaling is the only way to push its damage.

builds.name: Hyper-Armor Trade Bleed Rush
builds.why: The built-in Red Bear Hunt skill is a three-hit claw swipe with strong hyper armor, letting a tanky Strength build trade through enemy attacks while stacking bleed rapidly on bosses.
